title: Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA) + print-friendly output + translation-readiness tagging
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task makes the dual-reviewed pages accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA), print-friendly (families value bedside printables), and tagged translation-ready for the ewing-info-translations follow-on. It is tooling/output, not new medical content. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Deliver, as a PR, the accessibility, print-friendly, and translation-readiness output pipeline so pages meet WCAG 2.2 AA, render to print-friendly PDF, and are tagged ready for ewing-info-translations.
acceptanceCriteria: Pages meet WCAG 2.2 AA structure (headings, reading order, alt text, contrast); print-friendly PDF output; tagged translation-ready for ewing-info-translations
Does not alter approved content meaning; preserves standing labels and provenance; CI green
